title: Component Usage Example (one JSONL line)
description: A single composition example for a component. Each line of a {Name}.usage.jsonl file conforms to this schema. Lines are independent: adding a new example does not edit surrounding examples. Cross-component references use the `fsds.<ContractName>` namespace, validated against the contract registry by the codegen's usage validator (refs + props + slots are checked there, not here — this schema only enforces shape).

name.description: Stable identifier for this example, unique within the file. Used as the example's anchor in docs and as a stable handle for tests. Suggested: kebab-case (e.g. "default", "with-trigger", "compact-with-icon").

description.description: Optional human-readable description of what this example demonstrates.

tree.description: Root of the composition tree. Single component reference at the top, with optional props and slots populated by nested trees.

treeNode.description: A composition tree node: exactly one top-level key matching `fsds.<ContractName>`, whose value declares the props and slots used at this position. The single-key shape mirrors the dot-notation refs already used by tokens (`{semantic.color.foreground.primary}`).

props.description: Prop name -> literal value. Keys must match real prop names on the referenced contract (enforced by the usage validator, not by this schema). Values are literals only — no expressions, no event handlers. `children` may be a string, a treeNode, or an array of strings/treeNodes for mixed-content children.

slots.description: Slot name -> child content. The child may be a tree node (the typical case — another component composed into the slot) OR a plain string (for slots that naturally hold flat text, like description or label). Keys must match anatomy parts on the referenced contract (enforced by the usage validator, not by this schema).

propValue.description: A literal prop value (string, number, boolean, or null) — OR, for the `children` prop only, a treeNode or array of treeNodes/strings. Schema cannot enforce the children-specific shape per prop name, so we accept the union and let the usage validator narrow.
